name: Uttarakhand (उत्तराखंड / Devbhumi)
summary: Uttarakhand (उत्तराखंड in Hindi, also known as Devbhumi 'Land of Gods') is a Northern Indian Himalayan mixed common-law/Uniform Civil Code (UCC) constituent state of the Indian Republic — structurally distinctive globally as the only Indian state (other than Goa) operating a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) framework following the Uttarakhand Uniform Civil Code Act 2024 (effective 27 January 2025), making it the only Indian state to deliberately adopt a UCC framework (Goa's UCC framework is inherited from Portuguese Codigo Civil 1867 framework, not deliberate post-Independence adoption — Uttarakhand's UCC is the first deliberate post-Independence Indian UCC framework), as one of three Indian states formed in November 2000 via formal state-formation framework (Uttarakhand 9 November 2000 + Chhattisgarh 1 November 2000 + Jharkhand 15 November 2000), and as the central jurisdiction of the most sustained modern Indian-state-level statehood movement (Uttarakhand statehood movement 1979-2000 culminating in 9 November 2000 statehood under the Uttar Pradesh Reorganisation Act 2000). Uttarakhand was carved out of Uttar Pradesh and comprises ~53,483 km² with a population of ~10 million across two Himalayan divisions (Garhwal and Kumaon). Family-law framework operates under the Uttarakhand UCC Act 2024 for residents (effective 27 January 2025) + federal Indian common-law framework with UCC-specific UCC-Specific Marriage and Divorce framework, UCC-Specific Live-in Relationship framework (only Indian state requiring formal registration of live-in relationships), UCC-Specific Inheritance framework, and UCC-Specific Maintenance framework. The Uttarakhand High Court (established 9 November 2000, separating from Allahabad High Court) is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Supreme Court of India. Uttarakhand is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. India is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party — Uttarakhand is governed by the Indian non-ratification status.

statutory_framework.citation: Uttarakhand Uniform Civil Code Act 2024 (effective 27 January 2025)
statutory_framework.title: Uttarakhand UCC Act 2024
statutory_framework.relevance: Uttarakhand State Act of 7 February 2024 effective 27 January 2025 — only Indian state to deliberately adopt UCC framework post-Independence + only Indian state requiring formal registration of live-in relationships.

statutory_framework.title: Uttar Pradesh Reorganisation Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Federal Act of 25 August 2000 effective 9 November 2000 establishing the State of Uttarakhand (originally Uttaranchal, renamed 2007) via bifurcation from Uttar Pradesh.

statutory_framework.title: Uttarakhand Renamed Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Federal Act renaming Uttaranchal to Uttarakhand effective 1 January 2007.

apex_courts.name: Uttarakhand High Court
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Uttarakhand — established 9 November 2000.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of India
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Uttarakhand.

professional_regulators.name: Indian Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI)
professional_regulators.role: Indian Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Uttarakhand.

anonymisation_convention: Uttarakhand family-court decisions are anonymised per Indian court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Sugauli + British-Garhwal-Kumaon-acquisition framework
key_developments.description: Treaty of Sugauli 2 December 1815 ending Anglo-Nepalese War — substantively distinctive Himalayan British-Garhwal-Kumaon-from-Nepal-cession framework. Foundational pre-1947-Independence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory and Garhwal-Kumaon-British-Indian-administration.

key_developments.title: Uttarakhand Kranti Dal founded + statehood-movement-beginning framework
key_developments.description: Uttarakhand Kranti Dal (UKD) statehood movement party founded 25 July 1979 — substantively distinctive Himalayan beginning-of-formal-Uttarakhand-statehood-movement framework. Foundational substantive 21-year sustained-statehood-movement framework persisting through 2000 statehood affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: India ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + Uttarakhand-applicability framework
key_developments.description: India ratified UNCRC 11 December 1992 with applicability to Uttarakhand region (then part of Uttar Pradesh) — establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within Indian-derivative-common-law framework. Foundational substantive child-rights framework integration affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Rampur Tiraha incident + Mussoorie firing + statehood-movement-intensification framework
key_developments.description: Rampur Tiraha incident 2 October 1994 + Mussoorie firing 7 September 1994 — substantively distinctive globally pivotal-violence-events-that-intensified-Uttarakhand-statehood-movement framework. Substantive demographic and constitutional-democratic-trajectory framework affecting subsequent 2000 Uttar Pradesh Reorganisation Act.

key_developments.title: Uttarakhand State + 27th-Indian-state-+-Uttar-Pradesh-Reorganisation-Act framework
key_developments.description: Uttarakhand formed as 27th state of India 9 November 2000 under Uttar Pradesh Reorganisation Act 2000 — substantively distinctive globally one-of-three-Indian-states-formed-November-2000-Uttarakhand-+-Chhattisgarh-+-Jharkhand framework. Originally named Uttaranchal. Foundational substantive 26-year Uttarakhand-statehood framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Uttarakhand High Court + state-formation-+-Allahabad-separation framework
key_developments.description: Uttarakhand High Court established 9 November 2000 separating from Allahabad High Court — substantively significant Himalayan post-state-formation-High-Court framework. Foundational substantive Uttarakhand-High-Court framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Renamed Uttarakhand + Uttaranchal-to-Uttarakhand framework
key_developments.description: Renamed from Uttaranchal to Uttarakhand effective 1 January 2007 under Uttaranchal (Alteration of Name) Act 2006 — substantively distinctive Himalayan Devbhumi-Uttarakhand-naming framework. Substantive cultural-administrative framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Uttarakhand floods + Kedarnath-disaster framework
key_developments.description: Uttarakhand floods 14-17 June 2013 + Kedarnath disaster — substantively distinctive globally Himalayan-flash-flood-+-Hindu-pilgrimage-disaster framework. Estimated 5,000+ fatalities. Substantive demographic and family-law-implementation disruption affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Chamoli glacier disaster + Rishiganga-Dhauliganga-flood framework
key_developments.description: Chamoli glacier disaster 7 February 2021 Rishiganga-Dhauliganga-flood + glacier-rockslide event substantively affecting Uttarakhand — substantively distinctive globally Himalayan-glacial-rockslide-disaster framework. Estimated 200+ fatalities + NTPC Tapovan Vishnugad Hydroelectric Project destroyed. Substantive demographic and family-law-implementation disruption affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory framework + pre-UCC-2024 framework.

key_developments.title: Uttarakhand UCC Act 2024 + first-deliberate-post-Independence-Indian-UCC framework
key_developments.description: Uttarakhand State Act 7 February 2024 adopting Uniform Civil Code framework — substantively distinctive globally first-deliberate-post-Independence-Indian-UCC framework. Goa's UCC framework inherited from Portuguese Codigo Civil 1867 framework, not deliberate adoption. Foundational pre-2025-UCC-effective framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Uttarakhand UCC effective + only-Indian-state-requiring-formal-registration-of-live-in-relationships framework
key_developments.description: Uttarakhand UCC Act 2024 effective 27 January 2025 — substantively distinctive globally only-Indian-state-requiring-formal-registration-of-live-in-relationships framework. UCC-Specific Marriage and Divorce framework + UCC-Specific Live-in Relationship framework + UCC-Specific Inheritance framework + UCC-Specific Maintenance framework. Substantive constitutional-democratic-trajectory framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

structural_findings: Uttarakhand operates a mixed federal Indian common-law + Uttarakhand UCC framework — places Uttarakhand in the Northern Indian Himalayan-state cluster.

Only Indian state (other than Goa) operating Uniform Civil Code (UCC) framework is structurally distinctive globally — only Indian state to deliberately adopt UCC framework post-Independence (Goa's UCC framework is inherited from Portuguese Codigo Civil 1867, not deliberate adoption).
First deliberate post-Independence Indian UCC framework is structurally distinctive globally — Uttarakhand UCC Act 2024 effective 27 January 2025.
Only Indian state requiring formal registration of live-in relationships is structurally distinctive globally — UCC-Specific Live-in Relationship registration framework.

One of three Indian states formed in November 2000 via formal state-formation framework is structurally distinctive within Indian state-formation cluster — Uttarakhand + Chhattisgarh + Jharkhand all formed November 2000.
Central jurisdiction of most sustained modern Indian-state-level statehood movement (Uttarakhand statehood movement 1979-2000) is structurally distinctive globally.
Uttarakhand High Court 2000 establishment is structurally distinctive within Indian state High Court establishment cluster.
editorial_notes: Uttarakhand jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 6 to 10 key_developments with full Treaty-of-Sugauli-1815-to-Uttarakhand-UCC-effective-2025 trajectory: 1815-Treaty-of-Sugauli-British-Garhwal-Kumaon + 1979-Uttarakhand-Kranti-Dal-founded + 1992-India-UNCRC-ratification + 1994-Rampur-Tiraha-+-Mussoorie-firing + 2000-Uttarakhand-State-+-Uttar-Pradesh-Reorganisation-Act + 2000-Uttarakhand-High-Court + 2007-Renamed-Uttarakhand + 2013-Uttarakhand-floods-+-Kedarnath-disaster + 2024-Uttarakhand-UCC-Act-adopted + 2025-Uttarakhand-UCC-effective.

Mixed federal Indian common-law/Uttarakhand UCC Northern Indian Himalayan Indian-state (Uttarakhand UCC Act 2024 effective 27 January 2025 + Uttar Pradesh Reorganisation Act 2000 + Uttarakhand Renamed Act 2006 + Uttarakhand statehood movement 1979-2000 + Uttarakhand High Court 2000 + Indian non-Hague-1980). Only Indian state (other than Goa) operating Uniform Civil Code framework globally + only Indian state to deliberately adopt UCC framework post-Independence + first deliberate post-Independence Indian UCC framework + only Indian state requiring formal registration of live-in relationships + one of three Indian states formed in November 2000 + central jurisdiction of most sustained modern Indian-state-level statehood movement.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Uttarakhand UCC Act 2024 (effective 27 January 2025) + Hindu Marriage Act 1955 + Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Northern-Indian + Himalayan + mixed-Indian-common-law/Uttarakhand-UCC + Indian-state-cluster + Uttarakhand-UCC-Act-2024-effective-2025-globally-distinctive + first-deliberate-post-Independence-Indian-UCC-globally-distinctive + only-Indian-state-with-live-in-relationship-registration-requirement-globally-distinctive + 1979-2000-Uttarakhand-statehood-movement-most-sustained-modern-Indian-state-level-statehood-movement-globally-distinctive + November-2000-three-state-formation-Uttarakhand-+-Chhattisgarh-+-Jharkhand + Treaty-of-Sugauli-1815-British-Garhwal-Kumaon + Rampur-Tiraha-+-Mussoorie-firing-1994 + Uttarakhand-High-Court-2000-Nainital + Uttarakhand-floods-+-Kedarnath-disaster-2013 + Devbhumi-Land-of-Gods-renaming-2007 + Indian-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
